title: Client Protocol — Communication Layer
description: Create a Protocol class for encoding and decoding JSON messages over TCP. Methods: encode(data) takes a dict and returns bytes (JSON string + newline delimiter), decode(raw_bytes) parses bytes back into a dict. Create a CommandCenterClient with methods: connect(host, port), send_message(msg_type, data) which sends a JSON message with type/data/timestamp fields, subscribe_channel(channel) which sends a subscribe request, get_messages() which returns any pending received messages, and disconnect(). Message format: {"type": "chat/task/file/system", "data": {...}, "timestamp": "ISO-8601"}.
test_code:
# Tests for Step 5: Protocol and CommandCenterClient
import json
import socket
import threading
import time

assert 'Protocol' in dir(), 'Protocol class must be defined'
assert 'CommandCenterClient' in dir(), 'CommandCenterClient class must be defined'

# Test Protocol.encode
data = {'type': 'chat', 'data': {'text': 'hello'}, 'timestamp': '2026-01-01T00:00:00'}
encoded = Protocol.encode(data)
assert isinstance(encoded, bytes), 'encode must return bytes'
assert encoded.endswith(b'\n'), 'encoded message must end with newline'

# Test Protocol.decode (roundtrip)
decoded = Protocol.decode(encoded)
assert isinstance(decoded, dict), 'decode must return a dict'
assert decoded['type'] == 'chat', 'roundtrip should preserve type'
assert decoded['data']['text'] == 'hello', 'roundtrip should preserve data'
assert decoded['timestamp'] == '2026-01-01T00:00:00', 'roundtrip should preserve timestamp'

# Test Protocol with various data types
complex_data = {'numbers': [1, 2, 3], 'nested': {'a': True, 'b': None}}
rt = Protocol.decode(Protocol.encode(complex_data))
assert rt['numbers'] == [1, 2, 3], 'Should handle lists'
assert rt['nested']['a'] is True, 'Should handle booleans'
assert rt['nested']['b'] is None, 'Should handle None'

# Test CommandCenterClient init
client = CommandCenterClient()
assert hasattr(client, '_connected'), 'Client should have _connected attribute'
assert client._connected is False, 'Client should start disconnected'

assert callable(getattr(client, 'connect', None)), 'Client must have connect()'
assert callable(getattr(client, 'send_message', None)), 'Client must have send_message()'
assert callable(getattr(client, 'subscribe_channel', None)), 'Client must have subscribe_channel()'
assert callable(getattr(client, 'get_messages', None)), 'Client must have get_messages()'
assert callable(getattr(client, 'disconnect', None)), 'Client must have disconnect()'

# Test CommandCenterClient connect/disconnect with a real server
server_sock = socket.socket(socket.AF_INET, socket.SOCK_STREAM)
server_sock.setsockopt(socket.SOL_SOCKET, socket.SO_REUSEADDR, 1)
server_sock.bind(('127.0.0.1', 0))
server_sock.listen(1)
test_port = server_sock.getsockname()[1]

client2 = CommandCenterClient()
client2.connect('127.0.0.1', test_port)
assert client2._connected is True, 'Client should be connected after connect()'

# Accept the connection on server side
conn, addr = server_sock.accept()

# Test send_message
client2.send_message('chat', {'text': 'hi'})
raw = conn.recv(4096)
msg = Protocol.decode(raw)
assert msg['type'] == 'chat', 'Sent message should have correct type'
assert msg['data']['text'] == 'hi', 'Sent message should have correct data'
assert 'timestamp' in msg, 'Sent message should have timestamp'

# Test subscribe_channel
client2.subscribe_channel('alerts')
assert 'alerts' in client2._subscriptions, 'Channel should be in subscriptions'

# Test get_messages returns list
messages = client2.get_messages()
assert isinstance(messages, list), 'get_messages must return a list'

# Test disconnect
client2.disconnect()
assert client2._connected is False, 'Client should be disconnected after disconnect()'

# Cleanup
conn.close()
server_sock.close()
acceptance_criteria: Protocol.encode() converts a dict to JSON bytes with newline terminator
Protocol.decode() parses bytes back into a dict
Encode/decode roundtrip preserves all data types
CommandCenterClient.send_message() sends JSON with type, data, and timestamp
CommandCenterClient.subscribe_channel() tracks subscriptions and notifies server
CommandCenterClient.get_messages() returns and clears pending messages
hints: In send_message, build: {'type': msg_type, 'data': data, 'timestamp': datetime.now().isoformat()}
For get_messages, use msgs = list(self._messages); self._messages.clear(); return msgs
